Package i5.cb.graph.diagram

Class DiagramClass

  • java.lang.Object
    • i5.cb.graph.diagram.DiagramClass
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ILangChangeable, java.io.Serializable

    public class DiagramClass
extends java.lang.Object
implements ILangChangeable, java.io.Serializable
    The DiagramClass maintains the associations between userObjects, their DiagramObjects and the other items associated with the userobjects. A userobject is here any Object that is supposed to be displayed in the GraphEditor. The items that may be associated with one userobject include the color of the edgeline (of corse only if the userobject is represented by a DiagramEdge), a DefaultStyledDocument, two Components, a JPopupMenu and a Shape. All these items are intended to be created by the DiagramClass according to the single userobjects and they are stored by the DiagramClass. One might want to use one DiagramClass instance for all userobjects beeing represented in the same GraphInternalFrame
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

      Getter for property graphInternalFrame.
      DiagramClassHashtableEntry getHashtableEntry​(java.lang.Object userObject)
      This protected method gets the DiagramClassHashtableEntry for a certain userobject; if there is no entry yet it creates a new empty one.

      Gets a DefaultStyledDocument that is associacted with a certain userobject

      The DiagramClass provides one PopupMenu that does not refer to a specific diagramObject but to the whole diagramDesktop.

      Tells whether a certain userobject is already associated with a JPopupMenu.

      Removes a userobject and everything that was associated with this userobject from the table.

      void resetAllEdgeStrokes​()

      void resetAllSmallComponents​()  
      void setHashTableEntry​(java.lang.Object userObject, DiagramClassHashtableEntry hashEntry)  
      javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument updateLang​(java.util.Locale loc)
      Updates the JPopupMenu and DefaultStyledDocument for every userobject currently registered in this DiagramClass, provided the userobject and the associated popupmenu implement the ILangChangeable Interface

    • Field Detail

      • htDiagramClass

        protected java.util.Hashtable htDiagramClass
        This hashtable stores DiagramClassHashtable entries. The keys are user objects.

      • multiPopup

        This popupMenu is invoked by a set of DiagramObjects.

        protected GraphInternalFrame graphInternalFrame
        Holds value of property graphInternalFrame.

      • getName

        public java.lang.String getName​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Get a (short) string representation of this object.
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value
        Returns:
        a String value

      • getEdgeStroke

        public java.awt.BasicStroke getEdgeStroke​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Gets the BasicStroke used to paint the edgeline of the diagramEdge belonging to this userobject

      • getToolTipText

        public java.lang.String getToolTipText​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Get a (long) string representation of this object. This string is used in the tooltip for the diagram object.
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value. The userobject we want to get the toolTipText for
        Returns:
        a String value. The toolTipText itself.

      • getPopupMenu

        public javax.swing.JPopupMenu getPopupMenu​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Gets the JPopupMenu for a certain userObject
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value
        Returns:
        a JPopupMenu value

      • getMultiPopup

        public GraphPopup getMultiPopup​()
        The DiagramClass provides one PopupMenu that does not refer to a specific diagramObject but to the whole diagramDesktop. The actionListeners in this menu should handle the current selection instead of a single diagramObject.
        Returns:
        the popupMenu for multiple Nodes

      • getInfoDoc

        public javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument getInfoDoc​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Gets a DefaultStyledDocument that is associacted with a certain userobject
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value. The userobject we want to get the infodocument off.
        Returns:
        a DefaultStyledDocument value. Should contain infos about the userobject

      • getEdgeColor

        public java.awt.Color getEdgeColor​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Gets the edgecolor associated with a certain userobject. This method requires the userobject to be represented by a DiagramEdge
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value.
        Returns:
        a Color value

      • getEdgeHeadColor

        public java.awt.Color getEdgeHeadColor​(java.lang.Object userObject)

      • getEdgeHeadShape

        public java.awt.Shape getEdgeHeadShape​(java.lang.Object userObject)

      • getSmallComponent

        public java.awt.Component getSmallComponent​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Return a small component for an user object. By default, this returns a DiagramLabel with the result of getName(java.lang.Object) as label.
        Parameters:
        userObject - any Object
        Returns:
        a Component. null, iff the userObject itself is a Component

      • getComponent

        public java.awt.Component getComponent​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Returns the (big) java.awt.Component for this object. This component is used when the small component is not shown. To be compatible with the graph editor which is implemented in JFC/Swing, the component should be a subclass of JComponent. This method may return null, but then getSmallComponent must return a value. By default, if the user object is a component the user object is returned. Otherwise, this method returns null or a value which is stored in the hashtable.
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value
        Returns:
        a Component value

      • getShape

        public java.awt.Shape getShape​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Get the shape for this user object. The shape is drawn, when the small component is shown, it is drawn as the background of the small component.
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value
        Returns:
        a Shape value

      • getDiagramNode

        public DiagramNode getDiagramNode​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Gets the DiagramObject for this user object. If the user object has been used for the construction of a DiagramObject, i.e. was an argument of the constructor, then this method should not return null.
        Parameters:
        userObject - the Object we want to get the DiagramObject for.
        Returns:
        a DiagramObject value

      • addUserAndDiagramObject

        public final DiagramObject addUserAndDiagramObject​(java.lang.Object userObject,
                                                   DiagramObject diagObject)
        Add an user object with its DiagramObject to the hashtable. If the user object is already in the hashtable with a DiagramObject as value, then the old DiagramObject is returned and nothing is changed.
        Parameters:
        userObject - an Object value
        diagObject - a DiagramObject value
        Returns:
        a DiagramObject value

      • remove

        public void remove​(java.lang.Object userObject)
        Removes a userobject and everything that was associated with this userobject from the table.
        Parameters:
        userObject - the userobject to be removed.

      • updateLang

        public javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument updateLang​(java.util.Locale loc)
        Updates the JPopupMenu and DefaultStyledDocument for every userobject currently registered in this DiagramClass, provided the userobject and the associated popupmenu implement the ILangChangeable Interface
        Specified by:
        updateLang in interface ILangChangeable
        Parameters:
        loc - the new locale
        Returns:
        a DefaultStyledDocument value

        an Enumeration value
